description_long: This is a low, linear wall constructed from uniformly stacked rectangular stone blocks, capped with a slightly uneven, solid stone slab. The stone blocks are arranged in horizontal courses, giving the wall a traditional, robust appearance. The surface of the wall shows subtle color variations, with shades of gray and hints of weathering, suggesting outdoor exposure. The top surface is relatively flat but not perfectly level, with a natural, rough-hewn finish. The ends of the wall are squared off, and there are no visible openings or decorative embellishments. This type of wall is commonly found in urban parks, along sidewalks, or as a boundary in landscaped public spaces.
description: A short, rectangular stone wall with stacked blocks and a rough, uneven stone cap, showing signs of outdoor weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wall, with visible stacked stone courses and a slightly uneven top surface.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow end of the wall, highlighting the thickness and the stacked stone pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the same stone arrangement and cap, with minor surface irregularities.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the wall's thickness and the vertical alignment of the stone blocks.
